rule_text: All equipment, including wiring, shall be arranged to facilitate its operation, inspection and maintenance and access to its connections.
cv_target: Equipment accessibility; clear working space around panels

rule_text: Equipment likely to require examination, adjustment, servicing, or maintenance while energized shall have clear working space in the direction of access to live parts, not less than specified in Chapter 729.
cv_target: Clear working space dimensions around electrical equipment

rule_text: Doors into electrical control panel rooms shall be marked with a plainly visible and legible sign stating 'electrical room' or similar approved wording.
cv_target: Sign 'ELECTRICAL ROOM' on door

rule_text: Labels or other suitable means of identification shall be provided to indicate the purpose of switchgear and controlgear, unless there is no possibility of confusion.
cv_target: Identification labels on switchgear and controlgear

rule_text: Where the functioning of switchgear and controlgear cannot be observed by the operator, and where this might cause a danger, a suitable indicator complying with IEC 60073 and IEC 60447 shall be fixed in a position visible to the operator.
cv_target: Status indicator lights on switchgear

rule_text: Every circuit shall be capable of being isolated from all live conductors. Each supply shall have a means of isolation.
cv_target: Isolation device present per circuit

rule_text: Suitable means shall be provided to prevent any equipment from being inadvertently or unintentionally energized.
cv_target: Lock-off or warning tag on isolation devices

rule_text: Emergency switching shall be provided where electrically powered equipment constitutes a hazard that needs to be rapidly disconnected from supply.
cv_target: Emergency stop button/switch at identified hazard locations

rule_text: Socket-outlets shall be provided in accordance with the installation requirements for the intended use of the building.
cv_target: Socket-outlets present per room function

rule_text: Electric appliances shall be marked with the rated voltage, rated current or power input, and the manufacturer name or trademark.
cv_target: Nameplate/marking on electric appliances

rule_text: If the luminaire does not provide connecting devices for connection to the supply, the connecting devices shall be terminals per IEC 60998 or DCL plug per IEC 61995 or installation couplers per IEC 61535.
cv_target: Proper luminaire connection device visible

rule_text: Only independent lamp controlgear marked as suitable for independent use shall be used external to a luminaire (IEC 60417-5138 symbol).
cv_target: Independent-use marking symbol on controlgear

rule_text: Luminaires installed in wet locations shall be installed so that water cannot enter or accumulate in wiring compartments, lampholders, or other electrical parts.
cv_target: Proper luminaire enclosure condition in wet areas

rule_text: In zones 0 and 1 hazardous locations, each luminaire shall be identified as a complete assembly for the zone and clearly marked to indicate the maximum wattage of lamps.
cv_target: Zone-rated label and max wattage marking on luminaires

rule_text: Wiring shall be so arranged or marked that it can be identified for inspection, testing, repairs or alteration of the installation.
cv_target: Wire identification markers/labels at terminals

rule_text: Where an electrical conduit is required, it shall be distinguished from a pipeline or other services.
cv_target: Conduit visually distinct from other services

rule_text: Identification of conductors by color for electrical installations shall be according to Table 51-2.
cv_target: Conductor insulation colors per Table 51-2

rule_text: Where an alteration or addition is made to an installation wired in old colors with an extension in new colors, alphanumeric marking for both old and new conductors at the interface shall be according to Table 51-2.
cv_target: Alphanumeric conductor markings at old/new color interface

rule_text: Where an alteration or addition has been made, a warning sign in Arabic and English shall be fixed at the interface between old and new installations on or near the consumer distribution board.
cv_target: Bilingual warning sign at distribution board for mixed color installations

rule_text: The protective devices shall be arranged and identified so that the circuits protected may be easily recognized; for this purpose it may be convenient to group them in distribution boards.
cv_target: Circuit identification labels on protective devices in distribution boards

rule_text: Where appropriate, diagrams, charts or tables per IEC 81346-1 and IEC 61082 shall be provided indicating the type and composition of circuits and the identification of devices performing protection, isolation and switching functions and their locations.
cv_target: Circuit diagrams/charts posted at distribution boards

rule_text: The symbols used in diagrams shall be chosen from IEC 60617.
cv_target: IEC 60617 standard symbols on diagrams

rule_text: Electric appliances shall be marked with the rated voltage, rated current or power input, and the manufacturer name or trademark.
cv_target: Appliance nameplate with voltage, current/power, manufacturer

rule_text: PV installation: DC circuits shall be identified at all points of access and at each end of every conductor.
cv_target: PV DC circuit identification labels

rule_text: Socket-outlets and vehicle connectors for EV charging shall be of a type identified for the purpose and clearly marked.
cv_target: EV charging socket-outlet type marking

rule_text: Battery rooms and enclosures shall have identification labels and warning notices indicating voltage, hazard, and access restrictions.
cv_target: Warning notice on battery room/enclosure

rule_text: Wiring systems shall be selected and erected so that they can withstand the ambient temperature (AA) expected at the installation location. Equipment shall be selected per Table 51-1 external influences.
cv_target: Wiring type appropriate for ambient conditions; no heat-damaged insulation

rule_text: Wiring systems shall be placed sufficiently far from heat sources or shall be protected against the effects of heat, including radiant heat, by shields or other suitable means.
cv_target: Shield/spacing between wiring and heat sources

rule_text: Wiring systems in locations with presence of water (AD) or high humidity (AB) shall be selected and erected to prevent water ingress or accumulation. IP rating marking shall be visible.
cv_target: IP-rated enclosures and conduit in wet/humid areas

rule_text: Wiring systems in locations with presence of solid foreign bodies (AE) shall have appropriate degree of protection (IP code) marked on enclosures.
cv_target: IP-rated enclosures where dust/solid foreign bodies present

rule_text: Wiring systems subject to impact (AG) shall be protected by location or by additional mechanical protection such as conduit, trunking, or armor.
cv_target: Mechanical protection on wiring in impact-prone areas

rule_text: Precautions within a fire-segregated compartment: wiring systems shall not reduce the fire integrity of the compartment. Fire barriers shall be maintained at penetration points.
cv_target: Fire barrier/seal at wiring penetrations through compartment walls

rule_text: Sealing of wiring system penetrations: where wiring systems pass through fire-rated walls or floors, the openings shall be sealed to maintain the fire resistance rating.
cv_target: Fire-rated seal at cable entry through walls/floors

rule_text: Proximity to electrical services: wiring systems for different voltage levels or different types of circuits shall be adequately separated or segregated.
cv_target: Separation between power and communication cables

rule_text: Proximity to non-electrical services: electrical wiring shall not be run in the same conduit, trunking or duct as non-electrical services unless effectively separated.
cv_target: No mixed electrical/non-electrical services in same conduit

rule_text: Cable management systems for power and information technology cables shall be arranged to minimize electromagnetic interference. Physical separation or use of separate cable trays is required.
cv_target: Separate cable trays/management for power vs IT cables

rule_text: Cables in metal cable-trays: preferred arrangement places power cables and signal cables on separate trays or with physical separation within the same tray.
cv_target: Cable arrangement pattern on metal cable trays

rule_text: Continuity of metallic system components: metallic conduit, trunking, and cable tray sections shall be effectively bonded and connected to maintain electrical continuity.
cv_target: Intact metallic bonding between conduit/trunking sections

rule_text: Wiring systems exposed to solar radiation (AN) and ultraviolet radiation shall be of a type resistant to such exposure, or shall be provided with suitable protection.
cv_target: UV-resistant cable sheathing or protective covering on outdoor wiring

rule_text: Wiring systems in seismic areas (AP) shall be installed with suitable supports and fixings to withstand seismic effects. Flexible connections shall be provided where necessary.
cv_target: Seismic bracing on cable trays and conduit

rule_text: Devices for automatic disconnection of supply (circuit breakers, fuses, RCDs) shall be provided for protection against electric shock in TN, TT, and IT systems.
cv_target: Circuit breakers/fuses/RCDs present at distribution board

rule_text: Devices for additional protection: residual current protective devices (RCDs) with rated residual operating current not exceeding 30 mA shall be provided for socket-outlets intended for use by ordinary persons.
cv_target: RCD (30 mA) present in circuit for general-purpose socket-outlets

rule_text: Monitoring devices shall be provided where required for protective measures, such as insulation monitoring devices in IT systems.
cv_target: Insulation monitoring device present in IT system installations

rule_text: Arc fault detection devices (AFDDs) shall be selected and installed in accordance with the requirements for locations with particular risk of fire, as specified in Section 42-2.
cv_target: AFDD present in fire-risk location circuits

rule_text: Devices for protection against overcurrent (OCPDs) shall be clearly identified with their rated current and breaking capacity markings visible.
cv_target: Rated current and breaking capacity labels on OCPDs

rule_text: Surge protective devices (SPDs) shall be installed where required by Section 44-3 for protection against transient overvoltages of atmospheric origin.
cv_target: SPD present at origin of installation (where required)

rule_text: SPDs shall be installed as close as practicable to the origin of the installation and to the incoming protective conductor connection. Connecting leads shall be as short as practicable.
cv_target: SPD mounted near main distribution board origin

rule_text: Where the distance between the SPD and the equipment to be protected is greater than 10 m, additional protective measures should be provided, such as an additional SPD close to the equipment.
cv_target: Additional SPD near equipment if distance > 10 m from origin SPD

rule_text: Isolating devices shall be clearly identified and indicate the isolated position. The off position shall be clearly indicated.
cv_target: ON/OFF position indicator on isolating devices

rule_text: Switching-off for mechanical maintenance: a device shall be provided to enable equipment to be switched off safely for mechanical maintenance, and shall be clearly identified.
cv_target: Mechanical maintenance switch with identification label

rule_text: Emergency switching: a device shall be provided for emergency disconnection of supply to any equipment that may give rise to danger. It shall be readily identifiable and accessible.
cv_target: Emergency switch/button (red, clearly marked)

rule_text: RCD type marking: RCDs shall be marked with their type designation (AC, A, F, or B) to indicate their behavior in the presence of DC components.
cv_target: RCD type label (AC/A/F/B) visible on device

rule_text: Bathrooms: zones 0, 1, and 2 are defined by dimensions from the bathtub or shower. Only equipment permitted in each zone shall be installed. Zone 0 = inside bathtub/shower; Zone 1 = above bathtub up to 2.25 m; Zone 2 = 0.6 m beyond Zone 1.
cv_target: No prohibited equipment in bathroom zones 0, 1, 2

rule_text: In bathroom Zone 0, no switchgear, controlgear, or socket-outlets shall be installed.
cv_target: No switches/sockets in bathroom Zone 0

rule_text: In bathroom Zone 1, only equipment specifically designed for Zone 1 may be installed. Socket-outlets are prohibited.
cv_target: No socket-outlets in bathroom Zone 1

rule_text: Supplementary equipotential bonding shall be provided in locations containing a bathtub or shower, connecting all exposed-conductive-parts and extraneous-conductive-parts.
cv_target: Supplementary bonding conductors visible in bathroom

rule_text: Swimming pools: zones 0, 1, and 2 defined. Zone 0 = inside pool; Zone 1 = above pool rim up to 2.5 m; Zone 2 = 2 m beyond Zone 1.
cv_target: No prohibited equipment in pool zones 0, 1, 2

rule_text: In swimming pool Zone 0, no switchgear or controlgear, including socket-outlets, shall be installed.
cv_target: No switches/sockets in pool Zone 0

rule_text: Junction boxes shall not be installed in swimming pool Zone 0. In Zone 1, only SELV junction boxes are permitted and shall have IPX5 minimum and openable only by key or tool.
cv_target: No junction boxes in pool Zone 0; only IPX5 SELV boxes in Zone 1

rule_text: Electrical equipment in swimming pool zones shall have at least the IP code according to Table 702-1 (IPX8 for Zone 0, IPX5 for Zone 1, IPX4 for Zone 2).
cv_target: IP rating mark on equipment in pool zones

rule_text: Medical locations: RCDs with rated residual operating current not exceeding 30 mA shall be provided for socket-outlets in medical locations of Group 1 and Group 2.
cv_target: RCD (30 mA) for medical location socket-outlets

rule_text: Medical IT system monitoring: insulation monitoring devices shall be provided for medical IT systems with visual and audible alarms.
cv_target: Insulation monitoring device with alarm in medical IT system

rule_text: Medical location circuits and equipment shall be identified to indicate their group classification (Group 0, 1, or 2) and safety service classification.
cv_target: Group classification label on medical location distribution boards

rule_text: EV charging: socket-outlets and vehicle connectors for electric vehicle charging shall be of a type specifically identified for EV charging purposes.
cv_target: EV-rated socket-outlet/connector at charging point

rule_text: EV charging circuits shall be provided with a means of isolation accessible to the user.
cv_target: Isolation device at EV charging station

rule_text: Hazardous locations: zones shall be classified (Zone 0, 1, 2 for gas; Zone 20, 21, 22 for dust) and equipment shall bear marking appropriate to the zone classification.
cv_target: Zone-classified equipment marking in hazardous areas

rule_text: Wiring systems in hazardous locations shall be of a type specified for the zone. Cable entries into enclosures shall maintain the type of protection (e.g., flameproof, increased safety).
cv_target: Correct cable entry/enclosure type in hazardous zones

rule_text: Where provided, earth electrodes within an installation shall be connected to the main earthing terminal using an earthing conductor.
cv_target: Earthing conductor from electrode to MET

rule_text: A main earthing terminal (MET) shall be provided in every installation and shall be accessible for connection and testing.
cv_target: Main earthing terminal present and accessible

rule_text: Protective conductors shall have green-and-yellow color identification throughout their length. PEN conductors shall be identified by blue marking at terminations in addition to green-yellow.
cv_target: Green-yellow PE conductor; blue-marked PEN conductor

rule_text: Protective conductors shall be routed so as to minimize the possibility of damage or inadvertent disconnection during normal use or maintenance.
cv_target: PE conductor routing and physical protection

rule_text: Protective bonding conductors for connection to the main earthing terminal shall connect all extraneous-conductive-parts including water pipes, gas pipes, heating systems, and air-conditioning.
cv_target: Bonding conductors on water/gas/heating/AC pipes

rule_text: Protective bonding conductors for supplementary bonding shall be provided where required by the protective measures (e.g., bathrooms, medical locations).
cv_target: Supplementary bonding conductors in special locations

rule_text: Earth electrodes shall be of a type and installed in a manner that provides a reliable connection to earth. Concrete-embedded foundation earth electrodes shall be at least 5 cm deep in concrete.
cv_target: Foundation earth electrode properly embedded in concrete

rule_text: The main earthing terminal shall be connected to: foundation earth electrode or soil-embedded earth electrode, earth electrode for lightning protection system (if any), and metallic water/gas/heating service pipes entering the building.
cv_target: MET connections to electrodes and services

rule_text: Earthing conductors shall be identified and accessible for inspection and testing. Connection points shall be accessible.
cv_target: Earthing conductor identification and accessible connections

rule_text: Functional earthing and functional-equipotential-bonding for ICT equipment shall be provided where required, with a main functional earthing terminal (MFET) if needed.
cv_target: Functional earthing terminal for ICT equipment

rule_text: Emergency lighting applications: emergency lighting shall be provided in locations where loss of normal lighting could cause danger. Emergency luminaires shall be clearly identifiable.
cv_target: Emergency luminaire presence and identification

rule_text: Fire protection applications or equipment: electrical supplies for fire pumps, fire detection and alarm systems, smoke control systems shall be independently supplied and clearly identified.
cv_target: Fire protection equipment supply identification

rule_text: Wiring systems for safety services shall be selected and erected to maintain functionality for the required duration under fire conditions. Fire-resistant cables shall be used where specified.
cv_target: Fire-rated cable for safety service circuits

rule_text: Fire switch: where a fire switch is required, it shall be red, clearly labeled, and installed at a readily accessible location near the main entrance to the building or area served.
cv_target: Red fire switch near main entrance with label

rule_text: Electrical sources for safety services shall be independent of the normal supply and clearly identified. Standby generators or battery systems shall be installed in dedicated locations.
cv_target: Identified safety service power source (generator/battery)

rule_text: Electrical circuits of safety services shall be independently identified and segregated from other circuits throughout the installation.
cv_target: Safety service circuit identification and segregation

rule_text: Fire detection and alarm systems in communal facilities and workplaces shall be provided and shall be clearly identifiable.
cv_target: Fire alarm devices (detectors, call points, sounders) visible in communal areas

rule_text: Medical locations: safety service electrical supplies for medical locations shall be provided per group classification. Group 2 locations require uninterruptible power supply for safety lighting and medical IT systems.
cv_target: UPS/safety supply equipment in medical Group 2 locations

rule_text: Electrical equipment shall be selected and erected in accordance with the requirements of Table 51-1 indicating the characteristics of equipment according to the external influences. IP code marking shall be visible on enclosures.
cv_target: IP code mark on electrical enclosures

rule_text: Luminaires installed in wet locations shall have at least IPX4 unless otherwise specified. IP marking shall be visible on the luminaire.
cv_target: IPX4+ marking on luminaires in wet locations

rule_text: Swimming pool and fountain equipment shall have at least the IP code according to Table 702-1 (Zone 0: IPX8; Zone 1: IPX5; Zone 2: IPX4).
cv_target: IP rating mark on pool/fountain zone equipment

rule_text: Construction and demolition site installations: equipment shall have a degree of protection of at least IP44.
cv_target: IP44+ marking on construction site equipment

rule_text: Agricultural and horticultural premises: equipment shall have a degree of protection of at least IP44 for indoor locations and IP54 for outdoor locations.
cv_target: IP44/IP54 marking on agricultural premises equipment

rule_text: Medical locations: equipment shall have at least IPX1 for Group 0 locations and IPX4 for areas where cleaning with water jets is used.
cv_target: IP rating marking on medical location equipment

rule_text: Hazardous locations: equipment shall bear the appropriate Ex marking and IP protection rating for the zone classification in which it is installed.
cv_target: Ex marking and IP rating on hazardous zone equipment

rule_text: If equipment does not have the characteristics relevant to the external influences of its location, it may be used provided it is provided with appropriate additional protection (e.g., additional enclosure). The additional enclosure shall be visually identifiable.
cv_target: Additional protective enclosure around under-rated equipment

rule_text: Initial verification inspection shall confirm: method of protection against electric shock is correctly implemented (barriers, enclosures, insulation visible).
cv_target: Protective measures against electric shock visibly implemented

rule_text: Initial verification inspection shall confirm: presence of fire barriers and other precautions against propagation of fire and protection against thermal effects.
cv_target: Fire barriers at cable penetrations present and intact

rule_text: Initial verification inspection shall confirm: selection, location and installation of suitable isolating and switching devices.
cv_target: Isolation and switching devices present and correctly located

rule_text: Initial verification inspection shall confirm: selection of equipment and protective measures appropriate to external influences and mechanical stresses.
cv_target: Equipment type marking matching environmental conditions

rule_text: Initial verification inspection shall confirm: identification of neutral and protective conductors by color and/or marking.
cv_target: Correct conductor colors (PE: green-yellow, N: blue)

rule_text: Initial verification inspection shall confirm: presence of diagrams, warning notices or similar information.
cv_target: Diagrams and warning notices present at distribution board

rule_text: Initial verification inspection shall confirm: identification of circuits, overcurrent protective devices, switches, terminals, etc.
cv_target: Circuit labels on protective devices, switches, terminals

rule_text: Initial verification inspection shall confirm: adequacy of termination and connection of cables and conductors (no loose wires, proper lug connections).
cv_target: Proper cable terminations (no loose/missing lugs)

rule_text: Initial verification inspection shall confirm: selection and installation of earthing arrangements, protective conductors and their connections.
cv_target: Earthing arrangement and PE connections present and visible

rule_text: Initial verification inspection shall confirm: accessibility of equipment for convenience of operation, identification, and maintenance.
cv_target: Equipment accessible; no obstructions blocking access

rule_text: Initial verification inspection shall confirm: exposed-conductive-parts are connected to the earthing arrangement.
cv_target: Exposed-conductive-parts bonded to earthing system

rule_text: Initial verification inspection shall confirm: selection and erection of wiring systems (correct type, routing, support).
cv_target: Wiring system type, routing, and supports correct

rule_text: Electrical equipment having double or reinforced insulation (Class II equipment) shall be type tested and marked to the relevant standards, identified by the symbol IEC 60417-5172.
cv_target: Class II equipment symbol (double square) on electrical equipment

rule_text: Electrical equipment having basic insulation only with supplementary insulation applied during erection shall have the symbol IEC 60417 5019 fixed in a visible position on the exterior and interior of the enclosure.
cv_target: Supplementary insulation symbol on enclosure exterior and interior

rule_text: Electrical equipment having uninsulated live parts with reinforced insulation applied during erection shall have the symbol IEC 60417 5019 fixed in a visible position on the exterior and interior of the enclosure.
cv_target: Reinforced insulation symbol on enclosure exterior and interior

rule_text: All conductive parts separated from live parts by basic insulation only shall be contained in an insulating enclosure affording at least IPXXB or IP2X degree of protection.
cv_target: Insulating enclosure with IP2X/IPXXB protection around conductive parts

rule_text: Where lids or doors in the insulating enclosure can be opened without a tool or key, all conductive parts accessible when open shall be behind an insulating barrier (IPXXB or IP2X), removable only by tool or key.
cv_target: Insulating barrier behind opened door/lid preventing contact with conductive parts

rule_text: Conductive parts enclosed in the insulating enclosure shall not be connected to a protective conductor. PE terminals inside the enclosure shall be marked as PE terminals.
cv_target: PE terminal marking inside Class II enclosure

rule_text: Plugs and socket-outlets for FELV systems shall not be able to enter socket-outlets of other voltage systems, socket-outlets shall not admit plugs of other voltage systems, and socket-outlets shall have a protective conductor contact.
cv_target: FELV-specific plug/socket that is incompatible with other voltage systems

rule_text: Plugs and socket-outlets in SELV and PELV systems shall not be able to enter socket-outlets of other voltage systems and shall not have a protective conductor contact (for SELV).
cv_target: SELV/PELV-specific plug/socket incompatible with other voltage systems

rule_text: Flexible cables and cords in separated circuits shall be visible throughout any part of their length liable to mechanical damage.
cv_target: Visible flexible cable/cord run without concealed sections in damage-prone areas

rule_text: Where an IT system is designed not to disconnect on first fault, the occurrence of the first fault shall be indicated by either an insulation monitoring device (IMD) or a residual current monitor (RCM), with audible and/or visual signal.
cv_target: Insulation monitoring device (IMD) with alarm indicator in IT system

rule_text: Fixed equipment which may attain surface temperatures causing a fire hazard shall be mounted on/within or screened by materials that will withstand such temperatures, or mounted at a sufficient distance from any material on which temperatures could have deleterious thermal effects.
cv_target: Heat-producing equipment with clearance to combustible materials or heat shielding

rule_text: Where arcs or sparks may be emitted by permanently connected equipment, the equipment shall be totally enclosed in arc-resistant material, or screened by arc-resistant material, or mounted at sufficient distance for safe extinction of emissions.
cv_target: Arc-resistant enclosure or screening around arc-producing equipment

rule_text: Where electrical equipment contains flammable liquid in significant quantity (>25L), adequate precautions shall be taken to prevent the spread of liquid, flame and products of combustion (e.g. retention pit, sills, fire-resistant chamber).
cv_target: Retention pit or sill or fire-resistant chamber around equipment with flammable liquid

rule_text: Enclosures installed around electrical equipment shall withstand the highest temperature likely to be produced. Combustible materials are not suitable unless covered with non-combustible or not readily combustible material of low thermal conductivity.
cv_target: Non-combustible enclosure material around electrical equipment

rule_text: In conditions BD2, BD3, BD4 (difficult evacuation), switchgear and controlgear in passages shall be enclosed in cabinets of non-combustible or not readily combustible material.
cv_target: Non-combustible switchgear cabinet in escape route/passage

rule_text: In conditions BD3, BD4 and in escape routes, electrical equipment containing flammable liquids shall not be installed.
cv_target: Absence of equipment containing flammable liquids in escape routes

rule_text: Luminaires shall be kept at adequate distance from combustible materials. Spotlights and projectors: 100W or less min 0.5m, >100-300W min 0.8m, >300-500W min 1.0m. A luminaire with a lamp that could eject flammable materials shall have a safety protective shield.
cv_target: Luminaire clearance to combustible materials with protective shield for ejecting lamps

rule_text: Switchgear for protection, control and isolation in BE2 (fire risk) locations shall be placed outside unless in an enclosure providing at least IP4X, or IP5X in presence of dust, or IP6X for conductive dust.
cv_target: IP4X/IP5X/IP6X enclosure for switchgear in fire risk location

rule_text: PEN conductors are not allowed in locations where condition BE2 (fire risk) applies, except for circuits traversing such locations with no connection between the PEN conductor and any conductive part.
cv_target: Absence of PEN conductor connections in fire risk (BE2) locations

rule_text: Every circuit supplying equipment in BE2 locations shall be provided with a means of isolation from all live supply conductors such that no live supply conductor can remain closed when others are open (mechanically linked switch).
cv_target: Mechanically linked isolation device for circuits in fire risk locations

rule_text: Boxes and enclosures installed in prefabricated hollow walls liable to be drilled shall have a degree of protection of at least IP3X.
cv_target: IP3X box/enclosure in hollow wall

rule_text: Supporting parts, frames and enclosures of heating elements in forced air heating systems shall be of non-combustible material.
cv_target: Non-combustible frame/enclosure of forced air heating element

rule_text: Frame and enclosure of space heating appliances shall be of non-combustible material. Radiant heaters should be mounted with safety distance of at least 2m from flammable parts.
cv_target: Non-combustible enclosure of space heater and 2m clearance from flammable materials

rule_text: Metal sheaths of cables should be bonded to the Common Bonding Network (CBN).
cv_target: Bonded metal cable sheath connection to CBN

rule_text: Where a lightning protection system is installed, power and signal cables shall be separated from the down conductors of the LPS by either a minimum distance or by use of screening.
cv_target: Separation between power/signal cables and lightning protection down conductors

rule_text: Metal pipes and metal armouring of cables entering a building shall be bonded to the main earthing terminal by means of conductors having low impedance.
cv_target: Bonding connection from metal pipe/cable armour to main earthing terminal at building entry

rule_text: Functional earthing conductors shall not use the green-and-yellow color reserved for earthing conductors. It is recommended to use the same color throughout the whole installation to mark functional earthing conductors at each end.
cv_target: Functional earthing conductor color (not green-yellow, preferably pink)

rule_text: Where the specification of ICT cable is not available, the cable separation distance between power and ICT cables shall be not less than 200mm in free air.
cv_target: Minimum 200mm separation between power cables and ICT cables

rule_text: Equipment on the DC side of PV installations (PV modules, distribution boards, cabinets) shall be Class II or equivalent insulation according to IEC 61140.
cv_target: Class II or equivalent insulation marking on PV DC-side equipment

rule_text: Equipment in outdoor PV locations shall have a degree of protection of at least IP44 and a degree of protection against external mechanical impact not less than IK07.
cv_target: IP44 and IK07 rated equipment in outdoor PV installation

rule_text: A sign indicating the presence of a photovoltaic installation shall be fixed at the origin of the electrical installation, at the metering position (if remote), and at the consumer unit/distribution board to which the PCE supply is connected.
cv_target: PV installation warning sign at origin, meter position, and distribution board

rule_text: Each point of access to live parts on the DC side (distribution boards, cabinets) shall have a permanent marking bearing the text 'SOLAR DC - Live parts can remain energized after isolation'.
cv_target: Permanent 'SOLAR DC - Live parts can remain energized' marking on DC-side access points

rule_text: Disconnection devices shall be marked with an identification name or number according to the PV array wiring diagram. All switches shall have the ON and OFF positions clearly indicated.
cv_target: Identification label and ON/OFF position indicator on PV disconnection devices

rule_text: The PV array DC switch disconnector shall be identified by a sign affixed in a prominent location adjacent to the switch disconnector. Where multiple disconnection devices are not ganged, signage shall warn of multiple DC sources.
cv_target: Identification sign adjacent to PV array DC switch disconnector

rule_text: The identification of AC and DC circuits shall be such that they are clearly distinguishable from each other (e.g. by labels or different colored cables).
cv_target: Distinguishable AC and DC circuit cables (different colors or labels)

rule_text: A cable used for functional earthing in PV installations shall not be identified by the color combination green and yellow. It is recommended to use the color pink.
cv_target: Functional earthing conductor in PV (pink color, not green-yellow)

rule_text: Plugs and socket-outlets normally used for the connection of household equipment to low voltage AC power shall not be used in PV arrays.
cv_target: Absence of standard household AC plugs/sockets in PV array

rule_text: Connectors on the DC side of PV installations shall be especially suitable for the DC side. Connectors accessible to non-skilled persons shall be disconnectable only by key or tool, or installed within a lockable enclosure.
cv_target: PV DC-specific connector with key/tool lock or in lockable enclosure

rule_text: Where conductors enter a PV combiner box without conduit, a tension relief arrangement (e.g. gland connector) shall be used. All cable entries shall maintain the IP rating of the enclosure.
cv_target: Tension relief/gland connector at PV combiner box cable entries maintaining IP rating

rule_text: All PCEs should bear a marking indicating that, before any servicing operation, the PCE shall be isolated from all supplies.
cv_target: Isolation warning marking on PCE (power conditioning equipment)

rule_text: Equipment in sauna rooms shall have at least IP24 degree of protection. If cleaning by water jets may be expected, equipment shall be at least IPX5.
cv_target: IP24 (or IPX5) rated equipment in sauna room

rule_text: Socket-outlets shall not be erected within the location containing the sauna heater.
cv_target: Absence of socket-outlets inside sauna room

rule_text: Switchgear and controlgear for lighting (not part of sauna heater) shall be placed outside the sauna room or cabin.
cv_target: Switchgear and controlgear for sauna lighting mounted outside sauna room

rule_text: Metallic sheaths and metallic conduits in sauna rooms shall not be accessible in normal use.
cv_target: Inaccessible metallic sheaths/conduits in sauna room

rule_text: Each assembly for construction sites (ACS) shall incorporate devices for isolating the incoming supply, suitable for securing in the off position (e.g. padlocking facility or lockable enclosure).
cv_target: Lockable isolation device (padlock facility) on construction site assembly

rule_text: Cables should not be run across site roads or walkways. Where necessary, special protection against mechanical damage shall be provided.
cv_target: Mechanical protection for cables crossing construction site roads/walkways

rule_text: In agricultural or horticultural premises, electrical equipment shall have a minimum degree of protection of IP44. Where IP44 is not available, equipment shall be placed in an IP44-complying enclosure.
cv_target: IP44 rated electrical equipment or enclosure in agricultural premises

rule_text: Electrical equipment generally shall be inaccessible to livestock. Equipment unavoidably accessible shall be adequately constructed and installed to avoid damage by and minimize risk of injury to livestock.
cv_target: Electrical equipment positioned inaccessible to livestock or livestock-proof

rule_text: Radiant heaters in agricultural premises shall be installed at a distance of at least 0.5m from livestock and from combustible material unless a greater distance is specified by the manufacturer.
cv_target: Radiant heater minimum 0.5m from livestock and combustible material in agricultural premises

rule_text: Only electrical heating appliances with visual indication of the operating position shall be used in agricultural premises.
cv_target: Visual ON/OFF position indicator on electrical heating appliances in agricultural premises

rule_text: Isolation devices in agricultural premises shall be marked according to the part of the installation to which they belong. Devices for isolation and emergency stopping shall not be accessible to livestock.
cv_target: Labeled isolation device in agricultural premises inaccessible to livestock

rule_text: A plan indicating the location of all electrical equipment, the routing of all concealed cables, a single line distribution diagram, and an equipotential bonding diagram shall be provided to the user of the installation.
cv_target: Installation documentation/diagrams provided to user in agricultural premises

rule_text: In areas with fire risk and combustible dust coating, only luminaires marked in accordance with IEC 60598-2-24 (limited surface temperature) shall be used. Luminaires marked with 'not suitable for normally flammable surfaces' shall have IP54.
cv_target: IEC 60598-2-24 marked luminaire (limited surface temperature) in agricultural fire risk area

rule_text: Sources for SELV and PELV in conducting locations with restricted movement shall be situated outside the conducting location, unless they are part of the fixed installation within the location.
cv_target: SELV/PELV source positioned outside conducting location with restricted movement

rule_text: The source for electrical separation in conducting locations with restricted movement shall be situated outside the conducting location unless it is part of the fixed installation within the location.
cv_target: Isolating transformer/source positioned outside conducting restricted location

rule_text: Equipment in caravan/camping parks shall be selected with a degree of protection of at least IPX4 to protect against water splashes (AD4).
cv_target: IPX4 rated equipment in caravan/camping park

rule_text: Every socket-outlet in caravan parks shall comply with IEC 60309-2 and meet IP44 or such protection shall be provided by an enclosure. Socket-outlets shall be interlocked.
cv_target: IEC 60309-2 compliant interlocked IP44 socket-outlet in caravan park

rule_text: No more than four socket-outlets shall be grouped together in any one enclosure in caravan parks to maintain IP degree when in use.
cv_target: Maximum 4 socket-outlets per enclosure in caravan park

rule_text: The lowest part of any socket-outlet in caravan/camping parks shall be placed at a height between 0.5m and 1.5m from the ground.
cv_target: Socket-outlet height 0.5m-1.5m from ground in caravan/camping park

rule_text: Every overhead conductor in caravan/camping parks shall be insulated. Overhead conductors shall be at a height above ground of not less than 6m in vehicle areas and 3.5m in other areas.
cv_target: Insulated overhead conductor at minimum 6m/3.5m height in caravan park

rule_text: Equipment installed on or above a jetty/wharf/pier/pontoon in marinas shall be selected: IPX4 for water splashes, IPX5 for water jets, IPX6 for water waves.
cv_target: IPX4/IPX5/IPX6 rated equipment on marina jetty/pontoon

rule_text: Every socket-outlet in marinas (up to 63A) shall comply with IEC 60309-2 and meet IP44, or IPX5/IPX6 where AD5/AD6 applies.
cv_target: IEC 60309-2 compliant IP44 socket-outlet in marina

rule_text: Every socket-outlet on fixed jetties/piers and floating pontoons in marinas shall be placed to avoid the effect of splashing and/or submersion.
cv_target: Socket-outlet positioned to avoid splashing/submersion on marina jetty/pontoon

rule_text: All overhead conductors in marinas shall be insulated. Overhead conductors shall be at a height above ground of not less than 6m in vehicle areas and 3.5m in other areas.
cv_target: Insulated overhead conductor at minimum 6m/3.5m height in marina

rule_text: No more than four socket-outlets shall be grouped together in any one enclosure in marinas.
cv_target: Maximum 4 socket-outlets per enclosure in marina

rule_text: Control and protective switchgear in exhibitions shall be placed in closed cabinets which can only be opened by the use of a key or a tool, except for parts designed to be operated by ordinary persons.
cv_target: Key/tool-locked cabinet for switchgear in exhibition/show

rule_text: Lighting equipment with high temperature surfaces (incandescent lamps, spotlights, projectors) shall be suitably guarded and arranged well away from combustible material to prevent contact.
cv_target: High-temperature lighting guarded and spaced from combustible material in exhibition

rule_text: Luminaires mounted below 2.5m (arm's reach) from floor level in exhibitions shall be firmly fixed and sited or guarded to prevent risk of injury or ignition of materials.
cv_target: Firmly fixed/guarded luminaire below 2.5m in exhibition

rule_text: A separate circuit shall supply high-voltage signs/lamps in exhibitions, controlled by an emergency switch that is easily visible, accessible and marked.
cv_target: Marked, visible, accessible emergency switch for high-voltage signage in exhibition

rule_text: Flexible cables in exhibitions shall not be laid in areas accessible to the public unless protected against mechanical damage.
cv_target: Mechanical protection for flexible cables in public areas of exhibition

rule_text: Joints in cables in exhibitions shall be made using connectors or in an enclosure with at least IP4X or IPXXD degree of protection.
cv_target: IP4X/IPXXD enclosure for cable joints in exhibition

rule_text: Every separate temporary structure in exhibitions shall be provided with its own readily accessible and properly identifiable means of isolation.
cv_target: Readily accessible, identifiable isolation device for each exhibition stand/structure

rule_text: Enclosures of luminaires and lighting installations in external lighting shall prevent access to live parts without the use of a tool or key. All doors shall be lockable with a key or tool regardless of height.
cv_target: Tool/key-locked enclosure doors for outdoor luminaires

rule_text: For outdoor luminaires at a height of less than 2.8m above ground, access to the light source shall only be possible after removing a barrier or enclosure requiring the use of a tool.
cv_target: Tool-removable barrier for light source access on outdoor luminaire below 2.8m

rule_text: Electrical equipment in external lighting installations shall have at least IP33. Equipment mounted externally on lighting columns: at least IP44. Lanterns: at least IP54.
cv_target: IP33/IP44/IP54 rated equipment/lanterns in outdoor lighting installation

rule_text: Every circuit in external lighting installations shall be capable of being individually isolated from each of the live supply conductors.
cv_target: Individual circuit isolation device for each outdoor lighting circuit

rule_text: Metallic structural parts of buildings (e.g. pipe systems, parts of furniture) shall not be used as live conductors in ELV lighting installations.
cv_target: Absence of building structural parts used as live conductors in ELV lighting

rule_text: Bare conductors in ELV lighting (25V ac or less or 60V dc or less) shall not be placed directly on combustible material. At least one conductor and its terminals shall be insulated between transformer and protective device.
cv_target: Bare ELV conductors not on combustible material; insulated conductor between transformer and protective device

rule_text: Suspension devices for ELV luminaires shall carry 5x the mass of luminaires, but not less than 5kg. Insulation piercing connectors and counterweight termination wires hung over suspended conductors shall not be used.
cv_target: Adequate suspension device for ELV luminaire (5x mass, min 5kg), no piercing connectors

rule_text: Suspended systems with bare conductors in ELV lighting shall be fixed to walls or ceilings by insulated fixing means and shall be continuously accessible throughout the route.
cv_target: Insulated fixings and continuous accessibility for suspended bare ELV conductors

rule_text: If the identification of a protective device for a circuit in ELV lighting is not immediately evident, a sign or diagram (label) close to the protective device shall identify the circuit and its purpose.
cv_target: Identification label/diagram near protective device for ELV lighting circuit

rule_text: A permanent notice of durable material shall be fixed to the mobile/transportable unit in a prominent position, preferably adjacent to the supply inlet connector, stating the type of supply, voltage rating, number of phases, earthing arrangement, and maximum power.
cv_target: Permanent rating notice adjacent to supply inlet connector on mobile unit

rule_text: For socket-outlets individually protected by electrical separation in mobile units, a durable indication shall be located adjacent to these socket-outlets stating that only one item of equipment shall be connected to each socket-outlet.
cv_target: Warning label at electrically separated socket-outlets: 'one item of equipment only'

rule_text: Plugs on mobile/transportable units shall have an enclosure of insulating material. Plugs and socket-outlets located outside shall afford IP44. Appliance inlets shall provide at least IP55.
cv_target: Insulating plug enclosure, IP44 socket-outlets, IP55 appliance inlets on mobile unit

rule_text: The flexible cable connecting a mobile unit to supply shall enter the unit by an insulating inlet minimizing insulation damage risk. The cable sheath shall be firmly gripped by the cable gland or anchored to prevent stress on the termination.
cv_target: Insulating cable inlet and firmly gripped/anchored cable gland on mobile unit

rule_text: The use of the TN-C system is not permitted inside any mobile or transportable unit.
cv_target: Absence of TN-C system inside mobile/transportable unit

rule_text: The plug part for connecting the mobile/transportable unit to the supply shall be situated on the unit.
cv_target: Supply plug situated on the mobile/transportable unit itself
